news.title: Repco Home Finance's net profit falls 5.12% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Repco Home Finance reported a 5.12% dip in net consolidated profit to ₹103.15 crore for Q2 FY26, despite a 4.18% rise in total income to ₹441.90 crore. The company's loan book grew 7.7% to ₹15,033 crore, with GNPA ratio improving to 3.16%.

news.title: Ashiana Housing posts net profit of ₹27.54 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Ashiana Housing reported a net consolidated profit of ₹27.54 crore in Q2 FY26, a significant turnaround from a loss of ₹7.55 crore in the previous year. The company's total income surged by 195.95% to ₹176.18 crore. Ashiana Housing also acquired land in Chennai for a new senior living project with substantial sales potential.

news.title: SRG Housing Finance's net profit rises 24.85% in Q2 FY26
news.para: SRG Housing Finance has announced a robust 24.85% surge in net profit for the September quarter of FY26, reaching ₹8.24 crore. Total income also climbed significantly by 31.49% to ₹48.44 crore. The company's Assets Under Management (AUM) grew by approximately 33% year-on-year, demonstrating sustained growth and strong portfolio quality.

news.title: Emaar India to invest ₹1,600 crore to construct luxury housing project in Gurugram
news.para: Emaar India is investing Rs 1,600 crore in a new luxury housing project in Gurugram. The project, named Serenity Hills, will feature 997 apartments across seven towers. Construction is set to begin soon, with delivery expected in five years.

news.title: Avoidance transactions under IBC: What happens after insolvency resolution?
news.para: Indian insolvency law now allows recovery of assets from unfair transactions even after a company's resolution process ends.  Courts and new regulations confirm that legal actions to reverse preferential or fraudulent deals can continue. This strengthens the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code's aim to protect creditors and maximize value.  Recovered funds will benefit stakeholders.

news.title: Asian Paints' net profit rises 46.79% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Asian Paints reported a significant 46.79% surge in net consolidated profit for Q2 FY26, reaching ₹1,018.23 crore. This growth was driven by a robust 10.9% volume increase in its domestic decorative business, despite monsoon challenges. The company also saw a 6.45% rise in total income to ₹8,729.91 crore.

news.title: Ahmedabad: Demolition drive targets illegal constructions in Vatva, Jamalpur
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation's TDO led demolition drives in Vatva and Jamalpur, clearing 14 illegal structures encroaching on Bhoja Talav and a leased plot. Additionally, two toilet blocks and five compound walls were demolished in Lambha.  Solid waste management teams also inspected units, issued notices for littering, and seized banned plastic.

news.title: Keystone Realtors' net profit declines 84.91% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Keystone Realtors witnessed an 84.91% drop in net consolidated profit for Q2 FY26, reporting ₹9.89 crore compared to ₹65.55 crore in the previous year. Despite this, the company achieved a 10% year-on-year growth in pre-sales to ₹772 crore for the quarter, driven by new project launches and strategic business development.

news.title: Independent valuers to reassess prices of unsold sites in Mohali
news.para: Gmada is revising reserve prices for unsold properties by appointing independent valuers. This move aims to set realistic prices for sites that failed to attract bids in consecutive e-auctions, aligning them with current market conditions. The authority seeks to understand why certain residential and commercial properties remain unsold.

news.title: Crackdown on illegal PGs, hostels in Bengaluru from November 12
news.para: Bengaluru East city corporation is launching a major crackdown on illegal paying guest accommodations and hostels.  Health officers are now nodal officers, supervising teams to inspect trade licenses, fire safety, and hygiene. This action follows a report highlighting numerous PGs operating without proper permits, posing risks to public health and safety within residential areas.

news.title: Marathon Nextgen Realty's net profit rises 34.54% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Marathon Nextgen Realty reported a 34.54% increase in net profit for Q2 FY26, reaching ₹66.53 crore. This growth was driven by strong execution and financial discipline, despite a slight dip in total income. The company also launched a new phase of its Panvel township and raised ₹899.99 crore through a qualified institutional placement.

news.title: Centre invokes GRAP stage-III curbs in Delhi-NCR
news.para: In response to deteriorating air quality, Delhi-NCR has implemented rigorous anti-pollution protocols as part of Stage III of the Graded Response Action Plan. This decision, prompted by the alarming 'severe' status of the air, includes a suspension of all non-essential construction and an end to stone crushing and mining operations.

news.title: Bombay HC quashes order for NOC to filmmaker to merge four flats in Oshiwara society
news.para: The Bombay High Court has quashed a deputy registrar's order compelling a housing society to allow a member to merge four flats. Justice Amit Borkar ruled that the registrar exceeded his authority under the Maharashtra Co-operative Societies Act, as the power to compel NOCs for amalgamation is not vested in him.

news.title: Telangana HC suspends order setting aside e-auction of Budwel land
news.para: Telangana High Court has allowed HMDA to proceed with the e-auction of prime Budwel land, suspending a single judge's order. While the auction can proceed, the highest bidder won't be declared until the district collector decides on private parties' ownership claims. The court mandated a decision by December 12, with final auction steps after December 31.

news.title: Indian Hotels, ITC, EIH, others in race for Pune's Crowne Plaza
news.para: Indian Hotels, ITC Hotels, and EIH are among many companies interested in buying Crowne Plaza in Pune. The hotel is being sold through a bankruptcy court process. Over 40 companies have submitted expressions of interest. This sale is happening alongside another hotel, JW Marriott in Bengaluru, also under insolvency

news.title: OpenAI leases 50-seater office space with CorporatEdge in Delhi
news.para: US-based OpenAI has set up its first India office in Delhi, leasing a 50-seater workspace with premium flex operator CorporatEdge, according to two people familiar with the matter. The company had earlier announced plans to expand into India, and sources said it was particular about having a Delhi address, choosing not to locate in Gurugram or Noida.

news.title: Sri Lotus Developers and Realty's profit falls 8% to ₹46.36 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: In a recent financial report, Sri Lotus Developers and Realty Ltd revealed a dip in net profit by 8%, now standing at Rs 46.36 crore for the September quarter. Yet, the company experienced a positive turn with total income soaring to Rs 188.81 crore, reflecting strong operational performance.

news.title: Kolte-Patil Developers posts ₹11 crore loss in Q2 FY26
news.para: In a challenging September quarter, Kolte-Patil Developers faced a net loss of Rs 11.14 crore, alongside the news of Group CEO Atul Bohra stepping down. The company's total income plummeted by 50 percent, prompting the board to appoint a new additional director and begin the search for a new CEO.
